title: Kernelについて、soul.mdもしくはphilosophy.mdを作るべきか？方法としてはobsidianにある情報を抽出して作成する。マルチエージェント・オーケストレーションで点数化して評価せよ
summary_text: [High] Root cause is confirmed: `codex` is running from `~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0/bin/codex`, and the active package at `.../lib/node_modules/@openai/codex/` exists without its platform optional sibling `@openai/codex-darwin-arm64` (directory missing). The failure comes from that missing optional package, not from a corrupted script file. [High] Fix can be minimal and likely sufficient: install only the missing optional dependency into the same nvm global tree (not a full app reinstall first). Example: `npm install -g @openai/codex-darwin-arm64@npm:@openai/codex@0.115.0-darwin-arm64` (or install the exact same `@openai/codex` version’s `darwin-arm64` optional package that your current version expects). [Medium] `npm list -g` on `/Users/masayuki/.local/node-arm64/lib/node_modules` is empty, which indicates multiple npm global roots/envs; a generic `npm install -g` may target a different tree than the one that executes `codex`. If this environment is fixed once, rerun installation in the same Node prefix that owns `codex` (`~/.nvm/versions/node/v22.19.0`). [Medium] If the optional package install alone does not clear it, then fallback path is a clean reinstall of the global package in that same nvm prefix with optional deps enabled (i.e., `npm uninstall -g @openai/codex && npm install -g @openai/codex@latest`); this is broader than the minimal path. [Low] The error message’s suggested command (`npm install -g @openai/codex@latest`) is a valid recovery but is not the narrowest fix; it can mask prefix misalignment issues if shell environment later switches between npm roots.
